summary: Iteration 6: Full coverage testing of feature #1 — Analytics Dashboard. 18/19 backend tests passed (1 skipped due to no on-disk HLS file to exercise the view-recording hook). Frontend admin and viewer flows both pass: admin sees Analytics nav + page renders with all 6 stat cards, timeline range selector, top-videos sort selector, and empty state; viewer is denied (no nav item; direct /analytics navigation redirects to /).
passed_tests: GET /api/analytics/overview — admin 200 with all 7 expected fields (total_videos, ready_videos, total_views, unique_viewers, views_24h, storage_bytes, total_duration_seconds)
GET /api/analytics/timeline?days={1,7,30,90} — returns continuous daily series with N entries; each row has date+views
GET /api/analytics/video/{id} — admin AND viewer both 200 with required fields

Frontend (admin): nav-analytics visible, click navigates to /analytics, page renders all 6 stat cards (stat-total-views/unique-viewers/views-24h/videos/storage/duration), timeline-range select changes range, topvideos-sort select changes sort, topvideos-empty visible (no views yet)
Frontend (viewer): nav-analytics NOT present; direct goto /analytics redirects to / (Video Library)
skipped_tests: View tracking de-dupe via /api/stream/hls/{id}/playlist.m3u8 — skipped because no ready video with HLS file exists in test environment (only a PlayLab external video and a failed upload). Code path was reviewed: routes/videos.py:218-222 calls record_view() inside try/except, and routes/analytics.py:_hash_visitor()+30-min cutoff matches spec.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/analytics.py: timestamp comparison uses ISO-8601 string ($gt cutoff_iso). Works correctly because UTC ISO strings with offset are lexicographically sortable when stored consistently (record_view uses datetime.now(UTC).isoformat() which produces +00:00 suffix). Just be aware: if any code path ever inserts naive datetimes or different tz offsets, the de-dupe and timeline windows would silently misbehave. Consider storing as BSON datetime objects long term.

routes/videos.py:218-222: view recording wrapped in bare except — good defensive choice, but errors are silently swallowed (no logger). Add a logger.exception() so production analytics breakage is observable.
routes/analytics.py overview(): runs 6 sequential awaits (count_documents x3, aggregate x2, distinct, count). Could be parallelised with asyncio.gather() to roughly halve dashboard latency at scale.
routes/analytics.py /videos endpoint: query param 'sort=garbage' silently coerces to 'views' (no 400). Minor UX choice — fine, but documenting in OpenAPI would help.
AnalyticsDashboard.jsx: Promise.all triggers 3 parallel fetches every time `days` OR `sort` changes — overview reloads even when only sort changed. Minor inefficiency; consider splitting effects.
test_credentials: Admin StreamHost / TestPass123!@# and viewer testviewer / Viewer123! both verified working.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests are read-only on existing data (no TEST_ records created).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Analytics endpoints all behave to spec. The view-tracking de-dupe path could not be exercised end-to-end because the test environment has no ready HLS-encoded video. If a real video gets uploaded and processed in a future iteration, run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_analytics.py::TestViewTracking to validate the +1-then-deduped behaviour. Next features to implement (in order from the original 6-feature plan): 2) transcoding presets, 3) comments, 4) captions, 5) sharing links, 6) API rate limiting.
